title: In vitro culture of Microsporidia species for genome sequencing with minimal host contaminant
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"73dt0","text":"Microsporidia are obligate intracellular parasites infecting a wide range of hosts, including humans. They can cause severe infectious diseases if left untreated, particularly in immunocompromised individuals. The propagation of the human-infecting microsporidian species in vitro is essential for generating sufficient material for genomics studies, yet existing protocols often lack detail, accessibility, or strategies to minimize host DNA contamination. Here, we present an optimized, reproducible protocol for culturing Encephalitozoon spp. in human foreskin fibroblasts (HFF-1), designed to produce high-yield and high-quality genomic DNA with minimal host DNA for downstream sequencing experiments. In one month, our method yielded approximately one billion spores, which were purified using mechanical disruption, filtration, detergent treatment, and DNase I treatment to remove free host DNA. The reduction of host DNA was validated through PCR, and next-generation sequencing – with Illumina, PacBio and Oxford Nanopore - revealing that 83-97% of the reads mapped to microsporidian genomes. This protocol enabled the generation of the first telomere-to-telomere assemblies for E. cuniculi, E. hellem, and E. intestinalis. Our workflow provides a robust framework for producing microsporidian genomic material suitable for advanced genomics applications, with potential adaptability to other intracellular pathogens. ","type":"align-justify","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":272,"length":8},{"style":"italic","offset":525,"length":15},{"style":"italic","offset":1187,"length":11},{"style":"italic","offset":1200,"length":10},{"style":"italic","offset":1215,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Incubate petri dish at 37 °C , 5% CO₂ for at least 01:00:00
Remove the gelatin solution from the petri dish by suction

Leave petri dish open in the biosafety hood until dry (max 00:20:00 ) If gelatin is not dry after 20 min, quick use the suction to dry the wet spots; if petri dishes are open for too long it might damage the coating by over drying.
Close dishes and start using it for cell culture now
Add 10 mL of fresh enriched DMEM media to a gelatin-coated petri dish
Quickly thaw cell culture vial in water bath at 37 °C for 00:02:00, ( 1-2 min)
Resuspend cells in the vial by pipetting delicately

Transfer the entire content of the vial to the petri dish with media Cells should be added to the media and not to the bottom of the petri dish so they won’t get stuck and can spread throughout the plate.
Rock the petri dish to help cells distribute across the entire plate
Wash cells with 3 mL of PBS (1X, pH 7.4 ) twice
Discard PBS in 1:4 bleach waste
Add 3 mL of trypsin/EDTA (0.05%) to cells and incubate ( 37 °C at 5% CO₂) for 00:01:30
Check under inverted microscope to ensure cells are detached
Add 3 mL of fresh media to the plate to inactivate trypsin
Pass sample through a 27-G needle 3 times (up and down) with a 10 mL syringe
Pass the syringe-lysed cell suspension through a 5 μm PVDF filter onto a sterile tube
Pellet spores by centrifugation at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Remove supernatant and discard in a waste tube containing 1:4 bleach
Resuspend pellet with 1 mL of PBS-Tween 20 (0.3%, filter sterilized)
Centrifuge sample at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes and discard supernatant in 1:4 bleach waste
Repeat steps 10.10 – 10.12
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of PBS (1X)
Centrifuge tube at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Remove supernatant and discard in a waste tube containing 1:4 bleach
Repeat steps 10.14 – 10.16 for 7 times
Resuspend spores in 1 mL of PBS (1X)
Perform a spore count using a hemocytometer by transferring 10 µL of a 1:100 diluted cell suspension aliquot
Store spore suspension at 4 °C for over a year
Resuspend the 1 mL of spores/cell suspension in PBS by vortexing
Remove host-DNA contaminant
Add 15 µL of MgCl₂ solution ( 1 Molarity (M) , filter sterilized) to the sample
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:15:00 under rotation
Add 60 µL of EDTA ( 0.5 Molarity (M) , pH 8 , filter sterilized) and mix by inversion
Centrifuge sample at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes and discard supernatant
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of PBS (1X) and centrifuge at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Repeat step 12.7 5 times for a total of 6 washes
Resuspend spores in 1 mL of PBS (1X) and store sample at 4 °C .
DMEM (1X) + 10% FBS + 1% PSQ + 2 mM L-glutamine
Coating plates with gelatin prior to HFF-1 cell culture
Heat-inactivated FBS (fetal bovine serum) in water bath at 56 °C for 00:45:00
Thaw L-glutamine and PSQ (penicillin-streptomycin-glutamine) aliquots
Remove 60 mL of the DMEM (1X) media bottle
Add 50 mL of heat-inactivated FBS to DMEM bottle
Mix bottle by inversion and make aliquots
Add 5 mL of L-glutamine (200 mM, filtered)
Add 5 mL of PSQ (100X, filtered) to the DMEM bottle
Store at 4 °C
Starting HFF-1 cell culture from frozen stocks
HFF-1 cell culture passage
Passage is recommended when cells reach a 90% plate confluence This will ensure the cells grow in a monolayer
Remove and discard media from the petri dish
Wash cells with 3 mL of PBS (1X, pH 7.4 ) twice
Add 3 mL of trypsin/EDTA (0.05%) to cells and incubate ( 37 °C , 5% CO₂ ) for 00:01:30
Check if cells are detached under an inverted microscope
Add 3 mL of fresh media to the petri dish to inactivate trypsin
Incubate cells at RT for 00:02:30
Flush cells from plate, collect them and transfer to a conical tube
Centrifuge cells at 1000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Discard supernatant and resuspend cells in 1 mL of fresh media by gently pipetting
Split cells in a 1:4 or 1:6 ratio (recommend by ATCC protocol)
Transfer the selected volume of cell suspension to gelatin-coated plates (protocol 2) with 10 mL of fresh enriched DMEM media
Rock petri dish and check if cells are evenly distributed under a microscope
Incubate plates ( 37 °C and 5% CO2), after 01:00:00 check if cells are attached to the plates. Follow the media renewal protocol until passage is required again
Infecting HFF-1 cells with Microsporidia spores from ATCC
HFF-1 cell culture should be between 90-100% confluent at the time of infection
Quickly thaw vial with microsporidia sample in a water bath at 37 °C for 00:02:00, (1-2 min)
Gently resuspend the content of the vial by pipetting
Transfer the contents of the vial to the plate with the HFF-1 cells Make sure you add the spores to the media and not to the bottom of the dish M
Add media to the vial to recover most of the spores and transfer it to the petri dish

Media should be renewed 24:00:00 post-infection (Protocol 7 " Maintaining a HFF-1 cell culture infected with Microsporidia: Media renewal ") ATCC cryogenic media may contain DMSO, which should be removed from the cell culture to avoid HFF-1 cell toxicity
Incubate petri dish with infected HFF-1 cells at 37 °C at 5% CO₂
Rock the plate to ensure that microsporidian spores are properly distributed
Return petri dish to the incubator Cell culture should be checked daily to determine the need of media renewal
Check culture under microscope for confluence and adherence of infected cells
Add 10 mL of fresh enriched DMEM media to the cell culture
Remove all the 10 mL of media from the culture and transfer to a conical tube Follow protocol 8 " Harvesting microsporidian spores from supernatant " to harvest spores
Media should be renewed when it gets cloudy or the color changes to orange
Maintaining a HFF-1 cell culture infected with Microsporidia: Media renewal
Check if confluence of infected cells is lower than 90-100% Microsporidia are observable live in the cell culture under phase contrast microscopy
Harvesting microsporidian spores from supernatant
Collect cell culture supernatant into a 15 mL conical tube
Centrifuge supernatant-containing tube at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Store spore suspension at 4 °C for over a year
Resuspend pellet in 10 mL of PBS (1X)
Keep spore pellet. Discard supernatant in a waste tube with 1:4 bleach
Microsporidia infected HFF-1 cell culture passage
Passage is recommended when infected cells reach 90% confluence
Remove all media from petri dish and transfer to a conical tube (to harvest spores later, protocol 8)
Incubate dish at Room temperature for 00:02:30
Add 3 mL of fresh enriched DMEM to the cell culture
Add 3 mL of trypsin/EDTA (0.05%) to cells and incubate ( 37 °C , 5% CO₂) for 00:01:30
Wash cells with 3 mL of PBS (1X) two times and transfer to a conical tube (to harvest spores later, protocol 8)
Centrifuge sample at 1000 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes
Transfer detached HFF-1 cells/microsporidian spores to a tube
Resuspend cell suspension in 1 mL of fresh enriched DMEM
Discard supernatant in a tube containing 1:4 bleach
Incubate plates at 37 °C at 5% CO₂ Check cell culture daily for media renewal and passage needs

Transfer 100 µL of suspension per each gelatin-coated plate (total of 10 plates) containing 10 mL of fresh enriched DMEM media for a 1:10 split ratio The 1:10 split ratio is customized and could be adjusted accordingly
From infected HFF-1 cells (protocol per petri dish)
Remove media from plate and transfer to 15 mL conical tube (may have spores for harvesting, protocol 8)
From cell culture supernatant
Collect cell culture supernatant into a 15 mL conical tube
Replace media collected from cell culture with 10 mL of fresh media and place plate back in incubator ( 37 °C at 5% CO₂)
Check if cells are evenly distributed in the plate under an inverted microscope
Incubate petri dish with cells 37 °C , 5% CO₂

Check if cells are attached to the plate after 24:00:00 . If the cryo-media used to freeze the cells contains DMSO you may want to change half the media in the plate after 24h if cells are adhered to the petri dish.
Place plates back in the incubator ( 37 °C , 5% CO₂)
Add 5 mL of fresh media to the culture
Remove 5 mL of media from the petri dish
Check if cells are adhered to the petri dish and if confluence is increasing using an inverted microscope
Cell culture media should be renewed every 2-3 days
Maintaining a HFF-1 cell culture: Media renewal
Coat petri dish with 10 mL of gelatin from bovine skin (0.1%, autoclaved)
Add 10 µL of DNase I ( 10 mg/mL ) to the resuspended spores
Centrifuge the 15 mL tube containing media with spores at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Store sample at 4 °C
Count spores with the hemocytometer and 10 µL of the 1:100 diluted suspension
Resuspend spores in 1 mL of PBS (1X)
Repeat steps 11.14 – 11.16 for 7 times
Remove supernatant and discard in a waste tube containing 1:4 bleach
Centrifuge tube at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Resuspend pellet in 1 mL of PBS (1X)
Repeat steps 11.10 – 11.12
Remove supernatant and discard in a waste tube containing 1:4 bleach
Centrifuge tube at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Resuspend pellet with 1 mL of PBS-Tween 20 (0.3%, filter sterilized)
Remove supernatant and discard in a waste tube containing 1:4 bleach
Pellet spores by centrifugation at 1500 x g, Room temperature, 20 minutes
Pass the syringe-lysed cell suspension through a 5 μm PVDF filter onto a sterile tube
Pass sample through a 27-G needle 3 times (up and down) with a 10 mL syringe
Resuspend spores in 10 mL of PBS (1X)
Discard supernatant in a waste tube containing 1:4 bleach
creator: Anne Caroline Mascarenhas
